checkpoint_context.active_numbering_note: The active checkpoint path authorizes a bounded first-wave Ζ-A entry after Ζ-P0..Ζ-P2 while broader Ζ remains blocked.

transition_class_identifiers.description: A staged release or control-plane transition candidate whose meaning stays within explicit target, release, receipt, rollback, and refusal posture without claiming live runtime realization.
A bounded candidate whose validation posture depends on rollback-bearing continuity, cutover-sensitive evidence, or bounded runtime-adjacent change while still remaining non-live and proof-bearing.

A staged transition candidate that must remain compatible with trust posture, trust prerequisites, or revocation continuity without claiming live trust realization, revocation propagation realization, or publication execution.
A transition candidate that exists only to produce structural, compatibility, continuity, provenance, or proof-bearing validation outputs.

A candidate that crosses into authority handoff, state transfer, deterministic replication realization, proof-anchor continuity realization, live trust execution, publication execution, or shard relocation.

validation_class_identifiers.description: Validate subject, scope, intent, lifecycle posture, target context, and boundedness markers.
Validate release contract profile, release-index and resolution semantics, target applicability, and refusal law.
Validate reversible boundaries, bounded resulting state, refusal-aware restoration posture, and explicit non-reversible edges.
Validate operator transaction meaning, generalized receipt posture, and reconstructable evidence anchors rather than ordinary logs alone.
Validate compatibility with trust and revocation continuity doctrine without claiming live trust realization.
Validate bounded proof, continuity, refusal, or rollback-alignment packets that remain explicitly non-live.
Record that the candidate is blocked, partial, out-of-scope, trust-incoherent, proof-insufficient, or otherwise not lawfully valid for this family.

blocker_baseline_dependence.gate_freeze_dependency: This family remains subordinate to the Z-P2 first-wave perimeter and may not widen itself by convenience.

runtime: Isolation, coexistence, hotswap, and distributed-authority boundaries remain binding.
No candidate may hide truth in undocumented local state.
No validation packet may present replay, checkpoint, or cross-shard substrate as if broader distributed continuity were already solved.
release_control: Operator transaction doctrine remains upstream where relevant.
Release contract profile, release-index semantics, exact target context, and refusal law remain upstream.
Archive or mirror presence does not prove validation success.
Ordinary logs, CI traces, and filenames are insufficient as canonical validation evidence.
trust: Trust-aware posture may be validated only within non-realized first-wave bounds.
Live trust execution, live revocation propagation, distributed trust convergence, and publication execution remain out of scope.

doctrine_admission_validation_realizatio.doctrine: Defines what rollback-bearing staged transition validation constitutionally means.
doctrine_admission_validation_realizatio.admission: The family is admitted as the first bounded Ζ-A family under ready_with_cautions posture.
doctrine_admission_validation_realizatio.validation: The family may produce structural, compatibility, continuity, receipt, trust-aware, proof-only, or blocked/refusal validation outcomes.
doctrine_admission_validation_realizatio.realization: Live cutover, distributed transfer, trust execution, publication execution, deterministic replication realization, and proof-anchor continuity realization remain outside this family.

invalidity_failure_categories.description: The candidate crossed into transfer, trust execution, publication execution, or other later-wave territory.
Rollback-bearing posture is absent, ambiguous, or asserted without continuity support.
Proof, continuity, or refusal packets are too weak to support bounded validation claims.
Operator transaction or generalized receipt continuity is absent where required.
Trust-aware posture is contradictory, absent, or exceeds admitted first-wave bounds.
The candidate claims lawful runtime cutover, handoff, or distributed continuity beyond current doctrine.
The candidate or result cannot be reconstructed canonically across intent, review, proof, refusal, and outcome.
The candidate belongs to later-wave, blocked, or future-only families instead of this one.

canonical: upstream checkpoint, blocker, matrix, and gate doctrine
canonical validation packets, refusal packets, and proof packets later defined under this doctrine
canonical operator transaction, receipt, and provenance records where relevant

prohibited_scope_expansion_shapes: rollback-bearing claim with no continuity proof
bounded first-wave used to smuggle in distributed transfer
trust-aware validation treated as trust execution realization
unresolved blockers silently treated as solved
ordinary rehearsal or dry-run presented as rollback-bearing validation
code substrate local traces or dashboards treated as gate passage
first-wave admission rewritten as broader zeta readiness
downstream_refinement_prompts: later bounded Ζ-A family doctrine prompts
